data.title: BoJ minutes reveal board open to future rate hikes despite steady policy
data.description:
Minutes from the Bank of Japan's ((BoJ)) July policy meeting, released on Thursday, showed that while the board unanimously decided to keep interest rates steady, some members are advocating for future hikes to a "neutral" level. The discussion comes as the central bank monitors rising inflation and a recovering economy.

While some members believe underlying inflation is still below the 2% target, others feel that inflation expectations are already approaching or have reached that level.

One member noted that the current policy rate is "lower than the level deemed neutral," especially with prices remaining high and the output gap at zero.

Another member cautioned against being "overly cautious and miss the opportunity" to raise rates, particularly as stock prices responded positively to a new U.S.-Japan trade deal.

Several opinions within the board suggest a timely rate hike may be possible by the end of the year if the impact of U.S. tariffs proves limited.

This sentiment was further supported by a subsequent September meeting, where two board members officially dissented, calling for a hike to 0.75%. 

Investors now look to Friday’s Tokyo inflation data, a key guide for nationwide price trends, for additional signals on the policy path. 

The Nikkei 225 Index slipped 0.1% to around 45,600 while the Topix added 0.2% to 3,177 on Thursday, as Japanese equities lacked clear direction with technology stocks under pressure.

More on Japan

  • Japan Prime Minister Ishiba to step down

  • BoJ minutes reveal ongoing caution on economy amid inflation & market risks
  • Japan's manufacturing PMI revised upward to 48.7 in April
  • Japan to spend ¥388 billion on relief measures for U.S. tariff impact
  • Japan producer prices rise more than expected to 4.2% in March

data.title: Upexi reports FY results
data.description:
• Upexi press release (NASDAQ:UPXI): FY gross profit totaled approximately $10.7 million, representing a gross margin of 67%.
  • Net loss totaled approximately $13.7 million.
  • Revenue of $15.8M beats by $11.78M.

data.title: Federal Signal to acquire New Way Trucks for an initial purchase price of $396M
data.description:
Federal Signal (NYSE:FSS) said on Wednesday that it has  signed a definitive agreement to acquire Scranton Manufacturing Company d/b/a New Way Trucks, a U.S.-based designer and manufacturer of refuse collection vehicles, for an initial purchase price of $396M. 

As part of the acquisition, the company will also pay additional consideration of $30M for New Way's manufacturing facilities and associated real estate rights in Iowa and Mississippi. 

The initial purchase price assumes a cash-free, debt-free transaction and is subject to certain post-closing adjustments. In addition, there is a contingent earn-out opportunity of up to $54M, based on the achievement of certain specified financial targets over a two-year period.  

The transaction will be financed with a combination of cash on hand and availability under Federal Signal's credit facility. 

The company currently anticipates that its pro-forma net debt leverage ratio upon completion of the acquisition will be less than 1.5x.

The company expects the transaction to be neutral to earnings per share in 2026,. 

The company expects the transaction to be accretive to EPS in subsequent years, with anticipated EPS accretion of between $0.40 and $0.45 in 2028, including anticipated annual run-rate synergies of between $15 million and $20 million, currently expected to be substantially realized by the end of 2028.

data.title: Bolt Metals appoints Zachary Kotowych as CEO
data.description:
• Bolt Metals (OTCQB:PCRCF) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Zachary Kotowych as chief executive officer and a director of the company, effective immediately.
  • Kotowych brings nearly a decade of experience in the mining and capital markets sectors.

data.title: Kovo+ Holdings CEO resigns; Michael Steele appointed new CEO
data.description:
• Kovo+ Holdings (TSXV:KOVO:CA) announced on Wednesday the resignation of Justin Anderson, Chief Executive Officer, due to health reasons, effective immediately. 
  • Michael Steele, a current director of the corporation, will assume the role of chief executive officer effective immediately. 
  • Steele brings years of expertise in financing, mergers and acquisitions, and scaling companies.

data.title: Moderna opens manufacturing and R&D facility in the UK
data.description:
• Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) said on Wednesday that the Moderna Innovation and Technology Centre (MITC) at the Harwell Science and Innovation Campus in Oxfordshire, UK, has officially opened. 
  • The MITC will now produce British-made mRNA respiratory vaccines for the UK public to support the National Health Service (NHS) seasonal vaccination programs. 
  • Beyond respiratory diseases, the MITC will also support research into the potential of mRNA science in areas such as cancer, rare diseases, and immune disorders.
  • The site can produce up to 100 million mRNA vaccine doses per year, with the capacity to increase to 250 million in the event of a pandemic. The clinical R&D facility at the MITC also includes laboratories that will analyze samples from Moderna's clinical trials globally.

data.title: Transocean announces pricing of upsized public offering
data.description:
• Transocean (NYSE:RIG) announced on Wednesday the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 125M Transocean shares, par value $0.10, at a public offering price of $3.05 per share. 
  • The offering reflects an increase from the 100M Transocean shares originally proposed to be sold. 
  • The gross proceeds to Transocean from the offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses, are expected to be approximately $381.25M. 
  • Transocean intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for the repayment or redemption of indebtedness.

data.title: Cohu upsizes and prices 1.50% convertible senior notes offering
data.description:
• Cohu (NASDAQ:COHU) announced on Wednesday the pricing of $260M aggregate principal amount of 1.50% convertible senior notes due 2031. 
  • Cohu also granted the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $27.5M aggregate principal amount of the notes for settlement by October 3, 2025. 
  • The aggregate principal amount of the offering was increased from the previously announced offering size of $200M. 
  • The notes will be Cohu’s senior unsecured obligations. The notes will mature on January 15, 2031, unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased.
  • The notes will bear interest at 1.50% per year, payable semi-annually in arrears on January 15 and July 15 of each year, beginning on January 15, 2026.

data.title: PepGen announces pricing of $100M public offering
data.description:
• PepGen (NASDAQ:PEPG) announced on Wednesday the pricing of an underwritten offering  of 31.25M shares of its common stock at a price to the public of $3.20 per share. 
  • The aggregate gross proceeds to PepGen from this offering are expected to be $100M, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses payable by PepGen. 
  • PepGen currently intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to fund its ongoing research and clinical development efforts, including the FREEDOM-DM1 and FREEDOM2-DM1 clinical trials, as well as for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: KalVista prices upsized offering of $125M of 3.250% notes due 2031
data.description:
• KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) announced on Wednesday the pricing of its offering of $125M aggregate principal amount of 3.250% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031 in a private placement. 
  • The aggregate principal amount of the offering was increased from the previously announced offering size of $110M. 
  • The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of KalVista and will bear interest at a rate of 3.250% per year, payable semi-annually in arrears on April 1 and October 1 of each year, beginning on October 1, 2026.
  • KalVista estimates that the net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $120.8M, after deducting the initial purchasers’ discount and estimated offering expenses payable by KalVista.
  • KalVista expects to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes, including the commercialization of EKTERLY. 
  • KalVista may also use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering for investments in and acquisitions of other companies, products, or technologies in the future.
